preview.summary: Reigning AFCON champions Morocco, a top-10 side and 2022 semi-finalists, face Haiti in their first World Cup since 1974. Morocco's pace and quality on the flanks will test a spirited Haitian side savouring a rare return to the global stage.
preview.homeForm: Morocco are ranked eighth in the world, their best-ever position, and won the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations on home soil.
preview.awayForm: Haiti, ranked around 83rd, topped their CONCACAF group with three wins and two draws to reach a second World Cup.
preview.homeKey: Achraf Hakimi, captain and arguably the world's best right-back.
preview.awayKey: Duckens Nazon, Haiti's all-time leading goalscorer.
storylines: Haiti's first World Cup appearance in 52 years
Morocco aim to back up their 2022 semi-final run as genuine contenders
Brahim Diaz, AFCON's standout performer, looks to create for the Atlas Lions
preview.h2h: The sides have met three times, most recently a 2-1 Morocco friendly win in 2023.
